title: Class Methods
description: Give your objects the ability to actively do things.

slides.title: Methods 🏃
slides.content:
Methods are simply **functions** that belong to the class. That means that your instantiated objects can actually perform actions!

There are two ways to define functions that belong to a class:
1. Inside class definition
2. Outside class definition

slides.title: Inside Example
slides.content: A method defined inside the class is easiest.

quizData.question: Like with simple properties (`myObj.year`), what operator do we use to trigger an object's function from the outside world?
quizData.explanation: We use the dot `.` to peek inside an object's container to access both variables and methods.

slides.title: Outside Example 🧱
slides.content: Sometimes your methods are incredibly long. To keep your class `blueprint` at the top of the file clean and readable, you can declare the function name inside the class, and write the actual code outside of it!

slides.title: Writing it Outside
slides.content: To define the function outside the class definition, you have to specify the name of the class, followed by the scope resolution `::` operator.

quizData.question: A method is basically just a regular function that lives specifically inside an object.
quizData.explanation: Yes, 'Method' is the OOP word for a function owned by a class.
